title: WOS Studio (Authoring) — Effectiveness
description: Single composable Effectiveness object per studio-authoring/specs/effectiveness-and-applicability.md. Carries jurisdictional + temporal + appellate scope as one object referenced by SourceVersion, PolicyObject, Mapping, WorkflowIntent, and WorkflowElement via `effectivenessRef` — never copied. Composes parent `wos-delivery.schema.json#appliesWhen` FEL semantics. Single canonical home (this schema); referenced everywhere.
$comment: Promotion status (Stage-3, 2026-05-01): MVP shape; jurisdictions[] and temporalScope are load-bearing; appellateState + supersession refs are illustrative.

$wosStudioEffectiveness.description: Document marker pinning this artifact to the Studio Effectiveness 1.0 schema.

x-lm.intent: Document type marker for the Studio Effectiveness object

id.description: Stable Effectiveness identifier within the workspace. Referenced by `effectivenessRef` from PolicyObject, SourceVersion, WorkflowIntent, etc.

jurisdictions.description: Jurisdictions in scope. Each carries kind (federal/state/circuit/district/local/tribal/territorial/agency-specific) + code + optional displayName.

code.description: Jurisdiction code (ISO-3166 country/subdivision, FIPS, agency identifier, or other authoritative identifier).

intervals.description: Time intervals during which this Effectiveness applies. Each carries `start` and optional `end` (open-ended if omitted).

appellateState.description: Appellate disposition (final = court ruling stands; on-appeal = under appellate review; enjoined = court has stayed application; provisional = adopted but not yet final).

enjoinedScope.description: When appellateState = enjoined, the jurisdictions in which the injunction applies. Empty if not enjoined.

supersedingRef.description: When this Effectiveness has been superseded, reference to the superseding Effectiveness id.
